content.content:
title:	Coach server: standalone MCP app for developer guidance
state:	OPEN
author:	dlapiduz
labels:	status: needs-implementation
comments:	1
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	56
--
## Problem
All coding guidance (prompts, resources, standards) is hardcoded into the platform binary.
This couples infrastructure concerns with developer standards, making it impossible to run
the coding coach on a non-IAF platform or update standards independently of the platform.

## Users / Context
Enterprise operators who want to maintain coding standards separately from platform upgrades.
AI agents that need consistent coding guidance regardless of which deployment platform they use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New binary `cmd/coachserver/` that starts an HTTP MCP server
- [ ] Coach exposes all coaching prompts:
      language-guide, coding-guide, scaffold-guide, logging-guide,
      metrics-guide, tracing-guide (and future: license-guide, cicd-guide, security-guide)
- [ ] Coach exposes all coaching resources:
      iaf://org/coding-standards, iaf://org/logging-standards,
      iaf://org/metrics-standards, iaf://org/tracing-standards,
      iaf://languages/*, iaf://scaffold/*
- [ ] Coach has NO dependency on the K8s client or session management
- [ ] Coach configured via env vars: COACH_BASE_DOMAIN, COACH_ORG_STANDARDS_FILE,
      COACH_LOGGING_STANDARDS_FILE, COACH_METRICS_STANDARDS_FILE,
      COACH_TRACING_STANDARDS_FILE (so it knows about the platform it is running on)
- [ ] Coach is built via `make build` alongside existing binaries
- [ ] Existing `internal/mcp/prompts/` retains only platform-operation prompts:
      deploy-guide, services-guide, github-guide
- [ ] All coaching code moves to new `internal/mcp/coach/` package
      (sub-packages: prompts/, resources/, standards/)
- [ ] `make test` passes; existing test assertions for prompt/resource counts updated
- [ ] Dockerfile and Helm/manifest for deploying coach as an IAF Application added
      to `deploy/coach/`
- [ ] `docs/architecture.md` updated to describe the two-domain structure

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
The coach is stateless and read-only. No credentials, no K8s API calls, no session IDs.
A simple API key (`COACH_API_TOKEN`) should gate the HTTP endpoint to prevent unauthenticated
access to org standards (which may contain internal library or license policy details).
Flag with `needs-security-review` if the standards files can contain sensitive org data.

## Out of Scope
- MCP proxying from the platform to the coach (that is Issue 2)
- New coaching capabilities (those are Issues 3–6)
- Multi-tenant standards (one set of standards per deployment)

## Open Questions
- Should the coach MCP server require auth (Bearer token)? Recommendation: yes, same
  pattern as the platform `IAF_API_TOKENS` config.
- Should the coach's HTTP transport match the platform's streamable-HTTP approach or use
  plain request/response? Use the same transport for consistency.
---
title:	Platform MCP: proxy coaching requests to coach server
state:	OPEN
author:	dlapiduz
labels:	status: needs-implementation
comments:	1
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	58
--
## Problem
After #56, the coach is a separate app. But agents currently connect to a single
platform MCP endpoint. If we require agents to connect to two MCP servers, all existing
agent workflows break and agents need extra configuration.

## Users / Context
AI agents that should not need to know (or care) whether coaching guidance is served by
the platform binary or a remote coach service.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New env var `IAF_COACH_URL` on the platform (e.g. `https://coach.iaf.localhost`)
- [ ] On startup, if `IAF_COACH_URL` is set, the platform MCP server connects to the
      coach as an MCP client
- [ ] Platform re-registers each coaching prompt and resource from the coach, using
      forwarding handlers that proxy calls through to the coach client
- [ ] From the agent's perspective, all prompts and resources appear on the single
      platform MCP endpoint — no change to tool/prompt/resource names or URIs
- [ ] If `IAF_COACH_URL` is not set, coaching features are simply absent (graceful
      degradation with no startup error)
- [ ] If the coach is unreachable at startup, platform logs a warning but continues
      serving platform tools
- [ ] `serverInstructions` in server.go updated to explain the two domains when coach
      is configured
- [ ] Integration test: platform server with in-memory coach server proxies a
      `coding-guide` prompt call end-to-end
- [ ] `docs/architecture.md` updated with a component diagram showing the proxy flow:
      Agent → Platform MCP → Coach MCP

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
The platform-to-coach connection is internal (cluster-local). The platform should pass
its `IAF_COACH_TOKEN` as a Bearer token when calling the coach. The coach must not be
exposed directly to external agents without auth.

## Out of Scope
- Hot-reload of the coach proxy (restart required if coach URL changes)
- Load balancing across multiple coach instances

## Open Questions
- Does the go-sdk support registering forwarding handlers for prompts/resources read from
  a remote MCP client? If not, the platform must enumerate the coach's capabilities at
  startup and register proxy closures for each. The architect should verify the SDK API.

## Dependencies
Depends on #56 (Coach server: standalone MCP app for developer guidance)

title:	Test coverage: add tests for controller reconciliation and REST API handlers
state:	OPEN
author:	dlapiduz
labels:	bug, status: needs-implementation
comments:	1
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	16
--
## Problem
Two major components have zero test coverage:

1. **Controller (`internal/controller/`)** — The reconciliation logic that creates Deployments, Services, Traefik IngressRoutes, and kpack Images has no tests. The phase transition logic (`setBuilding`, `setRunning`, `setFailed`) is untested. kpack and Traefik interactions (unstructured object creation/update) are entirely unverified.

2. **REST API handlers (`internal/api/handlers/`)** — The full CRUD lifecycle for Applications, source upload, log retrieval, and the middleware chain (auth, session resolution, audit) have no tests.

This means bugs in the controller's reconcile loop (like the missing `Deploying` phase) can go undetected, and REST API regressions cannot be caught before deployment.

## Users / Context
- **All IAF contributors** — need a safety net when modifying controller or API code
- **The platform itself** — untested reconciliation logic is the source of the phase transition bugs already filed as separate issues
- **CI/CD pipeline** — `make test` currently passes even though these critical paths are unexercised

## Acceptance Criteria

### Controller tests (`internal/controller/application_controller_test.go`)
- [ ] Test: creating an `Application` with a pre-built image triggers creation of a `Deployment`, `Service`, and Traefik `IngressRoute` in the correct namespace
- [ ] Test: creating an `Application` with a git source triggers creation of a kpack `Image` CR and sets `phase: Building`
- [ ] Test: when the kpack `Image` CR's `latestImage` is set (build complete), the controller updates `Application.Spec.Image` and transitions to `Deploying`/`Running`
- [ ] Test: when the `Deployment`'s `AvailableReplicas` reaches the desired count, `phase` transitions to `Running`
- [ ] Test: `setFailed` is called and `phase: Failed` is set when kpack build fails
- [ ] Test: deleting an `Application` triggers deletion of owned resources
- [ ] All tests use the controller-runtime `envtest` package or the fake client; no real cluster required

### REST API handler tests (`internal/api/handlers/applications_test.go`)
- [ ] Test: `POST /api/v1/applications` creates an `Application` CR and returns 201
- [ ] Test: `GET /api/v1/applications` lists apps in the session namespace
- [ ] Test: `DELETE /api/v1/applications/:name` deletes the CR and source store entry
- [ ] Test: `POST /api/v1/applications/:name/source` stores files and updates the CR's `Blob` field
- [ ] Test: missing session ID returns 400
- [ ] Test: invalid Bearer token returns 401
- [ ] All handler tests use `httptest` and the fake K8s client

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
Tests must verify namespace isolation — a session must not be able to list or modify resources in another session's namespace. Add at least one cross-namespace access test that confirms a 403 or empty result.

## Out of Scope
- End-to-end integration tests against a real cluster (future)
- Performance/load tests

## Open Questions
1. Should controller tests use `envtest` (requires K8s binaries) or the fake client (faster, no external deps)? The fake client is preferred for CI speed.
2. Should API handler tests be table-driven (one test function with subtests) or individual test functions per endpoint?

content.content:
author:	dlapiduz
association:	owner
edited:	false
status:	none
--
## Technical Design

### Approach
Split coaching content (language guides, coding standards, logging/metrics/tracing) from platform-operation content (deploy, services, session management). A new `cmd/coachserver` binary backed by `internal/mcp/coach/` serves coaching prompts and resources via streamable-HTTP MCP with Bearer-token auth. The coach has zero K8s dependency. Platform keeps only deploy-guide, services-guide, github-guide. Issue #58 (proxy) re-exposes coaching on the platform endpoint so agents see no change.

Full design doc: `docs/architecture/issue-56-coach-server.md`

### Changes Required
- New: `cmd/coachserver/main.go` — binary, reads `COACH_*` env vars, Echo + MCP server
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/deps.go` — `CoachDependencies` (no K8s, no sessions)
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/server.go` — `NewCoachServer(deps)` wiring
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/prompts/` — move language-guide, coding-guide, scaffold-guide, logging-guide, metrics-guide, tracing-guide
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/prompts/aliases.go` — extract shared `languageAliases` map
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/resources/` — move org-standards, logging/metrics/tracing-standards, supported-languages, scaffold, standards-loader, defaults/
- New: `deploy/coach/` — Dockerfile + values.yaml
- Modify: `internal/mcp/prompts/` — delete 6 coaching prompts, keep 3 platform-only
- Modify: `internal/mcp/resources/` — delete 6 coaching resources, keep 4 platform-only
- Modify: `internal/mcp/server.go` — remove coaching registrations
- Modify: `Makefile` — add coachserver to build target
- Modify: test count assertions in prompts_test.go and resources_test.go

### Data / API Changes
**`CoachDependencies` struct:**
```go
type Dependencies struct {
    BaseDomain           string
    OrgStandards         *orgstandards.Loader
    LoggingStandardsFile string
    MetricsStandardsFile string
    TracingStandardsFile string
}
```

**Coach config env vars:** `COACH_PORT` (8082), `COACH_API_TOKEN` (required), `COACH_BASE_DOMAIN`, `COACH_ORG_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_LOGGING_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_METRICS_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_TRACING_STANDARDS_FILE`

**Auth:** Reuse `internal/middleware.Auth([]string{coachToken})` on Echo — same pattern as REST API.

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact
Coach is stateless and read-only. No K8s access, no sessions. Single deployment serves all tenants; this is intentional (standards are org-wide).

### Security Considerations
- `COACH_API_TOKEN` empty → refuse to start (exit 1). An unauthenticated coach leaks org standards.
- File path validation: `loadStandards` already rejects `..` — unchanged.
- Version strings in framework standards (#59): validate against `^[0-9a-zA-Z.\-+*x]+$` on load.
- Flag: **needs-security-review** for the empty-token startup guard.

### Resource & Performance Impact
One stateless Deployment. No apiserver load. Memory < 1 MB.

### Migration / Compatibility
**Rollout order matters:** until #58 (proxy) ships, agents will lose access to coaching prompts if the platform drops them. Recommended approach: keep local fallback registrations in the platform until #58 lands, guarded by `if cfg.CoachURL == ""`. Both issues can ship in the same release to avoid a regression window.

### Open Questions for Developer
- Confirm rollout strategy with PM: simultaneous release of #56 + #58, or staged?
- `languageAliases` is currently repeated in each guide file — extract to `aliases.go` as part of this work.
--

# Architecture: Test Coverage for Controller and REST API Handlers (Issue #16)

## Technical Design

### Approach

Add test files for the two major zero-coverage areas: the controller reconciliation loop and the REST API handlers. Both use the controller-runtime **fake client** (not envtest) to avoid requiring K8s binaries in CI. API handler tests use `httptest` with Echo. Tests are table-driven with `t.Run` subtests. The namespace isolation cross-namespace test is added as a mandatory security regression test. No new production code changes; this design is test-only.

### Changes Required

**New file: `internal/controller/application_controller_test.go`**
- Package `controller` (whitebox — same package as controller)
- Imports: `testing`, `context`, `github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/api/v1alpha1`, `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client/fake`, `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile`, `k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime`, `k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types`
- Helper `newScheme()` — registers `iafv1alpha1`, `appsv1`, `corev1`, and `unstructured` (for kpack/Traefik)

  - Non-Bearer format → 401
  - Invalid token → 401
  - `/health` path → no auth required (200)
  - `/sources/foo` path → no auth required (200)

### Data / API Changes

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact

The `ListApps_CrossNamespace` test codifies the namespace isolation invariant as a regression test. If someone removes `client.InNamespace(namespace)` from `handlers/applications.go`, this test will fail.

### Security Considerations

- **Fake client behavior**: the controller-runtime fake client does NOT enforce RBAC. 1. **`auth.NewSessionStore` signature**: confirm whether `NewSessionStore("")` or `NewSessionStore(t.TempDir())` is needed for in-memory-only use. If the store always tries to write a file, use `t.TempDir()`.

# Architecture: app_logs Multi-Replica Support and Consistent Pod Selection (Issue #15)

## Technical Design

### Approach

Fix pod selection in both the MCP tool (`RegisterAppLogsWithClientset`) and the REST API handler (`handlers/logs.go`) to consistently use the **most recently started pod** (sort by `CreationTimestamp` descending). Add an optional `pod_name` parameter that, when provided, fetches logs from a specific pod after validating it belongs to the app via label selector (preventing cross-app log access). Return `availablePods` in the response so agents can make targeted follow-up calls. Both the MCP tool and REST API handler are updated together since they share the same bug pattern.

### Changes Required

**`internal/mcp/tools/logs.go`**:

1. Update `AppLogsInput`:
   ```go
   type AppLogsInput struct {
       SessionID string `json:"session_id" ...`
       Name      string `json:"name" ...`
       Lines     int64  `json:"lines,omitempty" ...`
       BuildLogs bool   `json:"build_logs,omitempty" ...`
       PodName   string `json:"pod_name,omitempty" jsonschema:"optional - specific pod name to get logs from; if omitted, uses most recently started pod"`
   }
   ```

2. Add helper `selectPod(pods []corev1.Pod, podName string, labelKey string) (*corev1.Pod, []string, error)`:
   - `allPodNames`: collects all pod names from the pod list (capped at 10 — see open questions)
   - If `podName` is provided:
     - Scan the pod list for a pod with `Name == podName` **and** the expected label (`labelKey: appName`) present — validates the pod belongs to this app; if not found, return `fmt.Errorf("pod %q not found for application %q", podName, appName)`
   - If `podName` is not provided:
     - Sort pods by `CreationTimestamp` descending (`sort.Slice` on `pod.CreationTimestamp.Time`)
     - Return `pods[0]`

3. In `RegisterAppLogsWithClientset` handler:
   - Replace `pod := podList.Items[len(podList.Items)-1]` with call to `selectPod`
   - Pass `labelKey` and `input.Name` for the namespace isolation check
   - Add `availablePods` to result map: list of pod names (all pods matching the selector, capped at 10)
   - Build logs path already uses last item — change to use `selectPod` with sort-by-creation-desc (same behaviour, now consistent and documented)

4. Update `RegisterAppLogs` (degraded, no clientset):
   - Add `PodName` to input struct (accepted but ignored)
   - Response message can mention `pod_name` is not supported without a clientset

**`internal/api/handlers/logs.go`**:

1. `GetLogs` — replace `pod := podList.Items[0]` with most-recently-started-pod selection:
   ```go
   // Sort descending by creation timestamp
   sort.Slice(podList.Items, func(i, j int) bool {
       return podList.Items[i].CreationTimestamp.After(podList.Items[j].CreationTimestamp.Time)
   })
   pod := podList.Items[0]
   ```
   - Add `pod_name` query parameter: `podName := c.QueryParam("pod_name")`
   - If `podName != ""`, validate it belongs to the app using the same label check as the MCP tool
   - Return `availablePods` in response: `[]string{pod.Name, ...}` (all pod names, capped at 10)

2. `GetBuildLogs` — already uses `Items[len-1]`. Change to sort-by-creation-desc for consistency. Document this in comments.

**New `internal/k8s/pods.go`** (extract shared logic):
- `SelectMostRecentPod(pods []corev1.Pod) *corev1.Pod` — sort + return first
- `PodNames(pods []corev1.Pod, limit int) []string` — collect names up to limit
- `FindPodByName(pods []corev1.Pod, name, labelKey, labelValue string) (*corev1.Pod, error)` — scan + validate label

This avoids duplicating the same selection logic between the MCP tool and REST handler.

**Updated test file `internal/mcp/tools/logs_test.go`** (new or updated):
- `TestAppLogs_SinglePod`: existing behaviour unchanged
- `TestAppLogs_MultiPod_DefaultSelection`: returns most recently started pod (verify via `podName` in response)
- `TestAppLogs_MultiPod_SpecificPod`: `pod_name` returns that pod's logs
- `TestAppLogs_InvalidPodName`: pod not in app's label set → error
- `TestAppLogs_AvailablePods`: response includes `availablePods` list

**Updated test file `internal/api/handlers/logs_test.go`** (new):
- Same scenarios as MCP tool tests but via HTTP

### Data / API Changes

**`AppLogsInput`** — new optional field `pod_name`.

**MCP tool response** — new fields:
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "logs": "...",
  "podName": "myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2",
  "availablePods": ["myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2", "myapp-7d9b4c-bpfq8"],
  "phase": "Running"
}
```

**REST API response** (`GET /api/v1/applications/:name/logs`):
```json
{
  "logs": "...",
  "pods": 2,
  "podName": "myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2",
  "availablePods": ["myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2", "myapp-7d9b4c-bpfq8"]
}
```

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact

The `pod_name` validation (label check) is the critical isolation control:
- Agent requests logs from `pod_name: "other-agent-pod-xyz"` → not found in this app's pod list → returns error. No cross-app or cross-namespace log access.
- The validation must use `client.InNamespace(namespace)` + `client.MatchingLabels` — never skip the namespace filter.

### Security Considerations

- **`pod_name` injection**: validate against the pod list derived from the app's label selector, not via a direct `clientset.CoreV1().Pods(namespace).GetLogs(podName, ...)` call without validation. The validation step is mandatory.
- **Log content**: log content is user-app-generated; it may contain sensitive data. The platform does not filter log content — that is the agent/operator's responsibility. Document this in the prompt.
- No new RBAC changes needed (existing `pods/log get` permission covers any pod in the namespace).

### Resource & Performance Impact

- `sort.Slice` on O(replicas) pods: negligible.
- `availablePods` adds a small JSON field. Cap at 10 to bound response size.

### Migration / Compatibility

- `pod_name` is optional — existing callers unaffected.
- Pod selection change (from `Items[0]` → most-recent) is a **behaviour change**. For single-replica apps, behaviour is identical (one pod). For multi-replica apps, the returned pod may differ from before. This is the intended fix.
- No CRD changes, no env var changes.

### Open Questions for Developer

1. **`availablePods` cap**: 10 seems reasonable. Should it be configurable? No — hardcode 10 for simplicity.
2. **`availablePods` always vs on-demand**: issue asks whether to always return it or only when `pod_name` is not specified. Decision: always return it. It's a small field and always useful for agents debugging multi-replica apps.
3. **`sort` import**: add `"sort"` to imports in both files.
4. **`SelectMostRecentPod` edge case**: if all pods have the same `CreationTimestamp`, order is stable (sort preserves relative order for equal elements in Go's `sort.Slice` is not stable — use `sort.SliceStable`).

## Technical Design

### Approach
Split coaching content (language guides, coding standards, logging/metrics/tracing) from platform-operation content (deploy, services, session management). A new `cmd/coachserver` binary backed by `internal/mcp/coach/` serves coaching prompts and resources via streamable-HTTP MCP with Bearer-token auth. The coach has zero K8s dependency. Platform keeps only deploy-guide, services-guide, github-guide. Issue #58 (proxy) re-exposes coaching on the platform endpoint so agents see no change.

Full design doc: `docs/architecture/issue-56-coach-server.md`

### Changes Required
- New: `cmd/coachserver/main.go` — binary, reads `COACH_*` env vars, Echo + MCP server
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/deps.go` — `CoachDependencies` (no K8s, no sessions)
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/server.go` — `NewCoachServer(deps)` wiring
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/prompts/` — move language-guide, coding-guide, scaffold-guide, logging-guide, metrics-guide, tracing-guide
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/prompts/aliases.go` — extract shared `languageAliases` map
- New: `internal/mcp/coach/resources/` — move org-standards, logging/metrics/tracing-standards, supported-languages, scaffold, standards-loader, defaults/
- New: `deploy/coach/` — Dockerfile + values.yaml
- Modify: `internal/mcp/prompts/` — delete 6 coaching prompts, keep 3 platform-only
- Modify: `internal/mcp/resources/` — delete 6 coaching resources, keep 4 platform-only
- Modify: `internal/mcp/server.go` — remove coaching registrations
- Modify: `Makefile` — add coachserver to build target
- Modify: test count assertions in prompts_test.go and resources_test.go

### Data / API Changes
**`CoachDependencies` struct:**
```go
type Dependencies struct {
    BaseDomain           string
    OrgStandards         *orgstandards.Loader
    LoggingStandardsFile string
    MetricsStandardsFile string
    TracingStandardsFile string
}
```

**Coach config env vars:** `COACH_PORT` (8082), `COACH_API_TOKEN` (required), `COACH_BASE_DOMAIN`, `COACH_ORG_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_LOGGING_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_METRICS_STANDARDS_FILE`, `COACH_TRACING_STANDARDS_FILE`

**Auth:** Reuse `internal/middleware.Auth([]string{coachToken})` on Echo — same pattern as REST API.

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact
Coach is stateless and read-only. No K8s access, no sessions. Single deployment serves all tenants; this is intentional (standards are org-wide).

### Security Considerations
- `COACH_API_TOKEN` empty → refuse to start (exit 1). An unauthenticated coach leaks org standards.
- File path validation: `loadStandards` already rejects `..` — unchanged.
- Version strings in framework standards (#59): validate against `^[0-9a-zA-Z.\-+*x]+$` on load.
- Flag: **needs-security-review** for the empty-token startup guard.

### Resource & Performance Impact
One stateless Deployment. No apiserver load. Memory < 1 MB.

### Migration / Compatibility
**Rollout order matters:** until #58 (proxy) ships, agents will lose access to coaching prompts if the platform drops them. Recommended approach: keep local fallback registrations in the platform until #58 lands, guarded by `if cfg.CoachURL == ""`. Both issues can ship in the same release to avoid a regression window.

### Open Questions for Developer
- Confirm rollout strategy with PM: simultaneous release of #56 + #58, or staged?
- `languageAliases` is currently repeated in each guide file — extract to `aliases.go` as part of this work.

6. **Controller `setRunning` calls `Status().Update()`**: make sure the test scheme includes all needed types so the fake client's status subresource update works. You may need `.WithStatusSubresource(&iafv1alpha1.Application{})` on the fake client builder.

# Architecture: app_logs Multi-Replica Support and Consistent Pod Selection (Issue #15)

## Technical Design

### Approach

Fix pod selection in both the MCP tool (`RegisterAppLogsWithClientset`) and the REST API handler (`handlers/logs.go`) to consistently use the **most recently started pod** (sort by `CreationTimestamp` descending). Add an optional `pod_name` parameter that, when provided, fetches logs from a specific pod after validating it belongs to the app via label selector (preventing cross-app log access). Return `availablePods` in the response so agents can make targeted follow-up calls. Both the MCP tool and REST API handler are updated together since they share the same bug pattern.

### Changes Required

**`internal/mcp/tools/logs.go`**:

1. Update `AppLogsInput`:
   ```go
   type AppLogsInput struct {
       SessionID string `json:"session_id" ...`
       Name      string `json:"name" ...`
       Lines     int64  `json:"lines,omitempty" ...`
       BuildLogs bool   `json:"build_logs,omitempty" ...`
       PodName   string `json:"pod_name,omitempty" jsonschema:"optional - specific pod name to get logs from; if omitted, uses most recently started pod"`
   }
   ```

2. Add helper `selectPod(pods []corev1.Pod, podName string, labelKey string) (*corev1.Pod, []string, error)`:
   - `allPodNames`: collects all pod names from the pod list (capped at 10 — see open questions)
   - If `podName` is provided:
     - Scan the pod list for a pod with `Name == podName` **and** the expected label (`labelKey: appName`) present — validates the pod belongs to this app; if not found, return `fmt.Errorf("pod %q not found for application %q", podName, appName)`
   - If `podName` is not provided:
     - Sort pods by `CreationTimestamp` descending (`sort.Slice` on `pod.CreationTimestamp.Time`)
     - Return `pods[0]`

3. In `RegisterAppLogsWithClientset` handler:
   - Replace `pod := podList.Items[len(podList.Items)-1]` with call to `selectPod`
   - Pass `labelKey` and `input.Name` for the namespace isolation check
   - Add `availablePods` to result map: list of pod names (all pods matching the selector, capped at 10)
   - Build logs path already uses last item — change to use `selectPod` with sort-by-creation-desc (same behaviour, now consistent and documented)

4. Update `RegisterAppLogs` (degraded, no clientset):
   - Add `PodName` to input struct (accepted but ignored)
   - Response message can mention `pod_name` is not supported without a clientset

**`internal/api/handlers/logs.go`**:

1. `GetLogs` — replace `pod := podList.Items[0]` with most-recently-started-pod selection:
   ```go
   // Sort descending by creation timestamp
   sort.Slice(podList.Items, func(i, j int) bool {
       return podList.Items[i].CreationTimestamp.After(podList.Items[j].CreationTimestamp.Time)
   })
   pod := podList.Items[0]
   ```
   - Add `pod_name` query parameter: `podName := c.QueryParam("pod_name")`
   - If `podName != ""`, validate it belongs to the app using the same label check as the MCP tool
   - Return `availablePods` in response: `[]string{pod.Name, ...}` (all pod names, capped at 10)

2. `GetBuildLogs` — already uses `Items[len-1]`. Change to sort-by-creation-desc for consistency. Document this in comments.

**New `internal/k8s/pods.go`** (extract shared logic):
- `SelectMostRecentPod(pods []corev1.Pod) *corev1.Pod` — sort + return first
- `PodNames(pods []corev1.Pod, limit int) []string` — collect names up to limit
- `FindPodByName(pods []corev1.Pod, name, labelKey, labelValue string) (*corev1.Pod, error)` — scan + validate label

This avoids duplicating the same selection logic between the MCP tool and REST handler.

**Updated test file `internal/mcp/tools/logs_test.go`** (new or updated):
- `TestAppLogs_SinglePod`: existing behaviour unchanged
- `TestAppLogs_MultiPod_DefaultSelection`: returns most recently started pod (verify via `podName` in response)
- `TestAppLogs_MultiPod_SpecificPod`: `pod_name` returns that pod's logs
- `TestAppLogs_InvalidPodName`: pod not in app's label set → error
- `TestAppLogs_AvailablePods`: response includes `availablePods` list

**Updated test file `internal/api/handlers/logs_test.go`** (new):
- Same scenarios as MCP tool tests but via HTTP

### Data / API Changes

**`AppLogsInput`** — new optional field `pod_name`.

**MCP tool response** — new fields:
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "logs": "...",
  "podName": "myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2",
  "availablePods": ["myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2", "myapp-7d9b4c-bpfq8"],
  "phase": "Running"
}
```

**REST API response** (`GET /api/v1/applications/:name/logs`):
```json
{
  "logs": "...",
  "pods": 2,
  "podName": "myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2",
  "availablePods": ["myapp-7d9b4c-xkqj2", "myapp-7d9b4c-bpfq8"]
}
```

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact

The `pod_name` validation (label check) is the critical isolation control:
- Agent requests logs from `pod_name: "other-agent-pod-xyz"` → not found in this app's pod list → returns error. No cross-app or cross-namespace log access.
- The validation must use `client.InNamespace(namespace)` + `client.MatchingLabels` — never skip the namespace filter.

### Security Considerations

- **`pod_name` injection**: validate against the pod list derived from the app's label selector, not via a direct `clientset.CoreV1().Pods(namespace).GetLogs(podName, ...)` call without validation. The validation step is mandatory.
- **Log content**: log content is user-app-generated; it may contain sensitive data. The platform does not filter log content — that is the agent/operator's responsibility. Document this in the prompt.
- No new RBAC changes needed (existing `pods/log get` permission covers any pod in the namespace).

### Resource & Performance Impact

- `sort.Slice` on O(replicas) pods: negligible.
- `availablePods` adds a small JSON field. Cap at 10 to bound response size.

### Migration / Compatibility

- `pod_name` is optional — existing callers unaffected.
- Pod selection change (from `Items[0]` → most-recent) is a **behaviour change**. For single-replica apps, behaviour is identical (one pod). For multi-replica apps, the returned pod may differ from before. This is the intended fix.
- No CRD changes, no env var changes.

### Open Questions for Developer

1. **`availablePods` cap**: 10 seems reasonable. Should it be configurable? No — hardcode 10 for simplicity.
2. **`availablePods` always vs on-demand**: issue asks whether to always return it or only when `pod_name` is not specified. Decision: always return it. It's a small field and always useful for agents debugging multi-replica apps.
3. **`sort` import**: add `"sort"` to imports in both files.
4. **`SelectMostRecentPod` edge case**: if all pods have the same `CreationTimestamp`, order is stable (sort preserves relative order for equal elements in Go's `sort.Slice` is not stable — use `sort.SliceStable`).

title:	app_logs: support multi-replica apps and consistent pod selection
state:	OPEN
author:	dlapiduz
labels:	enhancement, status: needs-implementation
comments:	1
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	15
--
## Problem
The `app_logs` tool has two inconsistencies in how it selects pods:

1. **Runtime logs use the first pod (`Items[0]`)** — for a multi-replica app, this always returns logs from one arbitrary pod. If that pod is healthy but others are crashing, the agent never sees the failing pod's logs.

2. **Inconsistent selection between runtime and build logs** — runtime logs use `Items[0]` (first pod); build logs use `Items[len-1]` (last/most recent pod). The inconsistency is confusing and undocumented.

3. **No way to choose** — the tool accepts `lines` and `build_logs` but no `pod_name` or `pod_index`. Agents cannot request logs from a specific pod.

For agents debugging a crashing multi-replica app, this means they can only see one pod's logs, and that pod may be the healthy one.

## Users / Context
- **AI agents debugging failed deployments** — need to see logs from failing pods, not just the first pod in the list
- **Developers monitoring multi-replica apps** — want to inspect any replica on demand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `app_logs` runtime log selection changes to the **most recently started pod** (sort by `CreationTimestamp` descending, take `Items[0]`) — this is more likely to be the relevant pod when debugging a rollout or crash loop
- [ ] `app_logs` accepts an optional `pod_name` parameter; if provided, logs are fetched from that specific pod (validated against the app's label selector before fetching)
- [ ] `app_logs` returns the selected pod's name in the response so the agent knows which pod's logs it received
- [ ] When `pod_name` is not specified, the response includes a `availablePods` list of all pod names matching the app's label selector, so agents can make a targeted follow-up call
- [ ] Build log selection is changed to most-recent build pod (already uses last item; confirm and document this behavior)
- [ ] Tests cover: single pod (existing behavior), multi-pod with pod selection by name, invalid pod name returns clear error

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
`pod_name` must be validated against the app's label selector before fetching logs — an agent must not be able to specify an arbitrary pod name and read logs from a pod it does not own (e.g., a pod in the same namespace belonging to a different app, or in a different namespace). The label selector check enforces this.

## Out of Scope
- Log aggregation across all replicas in a single call (future)
- Log tailing / streaming (future — current implementation fetches a fixed number of lines)
- Structured log parsing

## Open Questions
1. Should `availablePods` always be returned, or only when `pod_name` is not specified?
2. Should we cap the number of pods listed in `availablePods` (e.g., max 10) for very high-replica apps?
---
title:	Session lifecycle: expiry, unregister tool, and namespace cleanup
state:	OPEN
author:	dlapiduz
labels:	enhancement, status: needs-implementation
comments:	1
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	6
--
## Problem
Agent sessions are permanent. Once created via the `register` MCP tool, a session and its Kubernetes namespace exist forever. There is no `unregister` tool, no TTL, no idle timeout, and no cleanup path. The `SessionStore` has no `Delete()` method.

In production, agents are short-lived — they run a task and exit. Over time, abandoned sessions accumulate: stale namespaces, orphaned `Application` CRs, leftover source tarballs, and unused kpack service accounts. This wastes cluster resources, complicates observability, and creates attack surface (an old namespace with running workloads but no owning agent).

## Users / Context
- **Platform operators** — need namespaces to be cleaned up automatically when agents are done
- **AI agents** — need a way to signal they are done and release resources
- **Multi-tenant clusters** — stale namespaces with running pods consume CPU/memory and inflate cloud bills
- **Security teams** — orphaned namespaces with old credentials are a risk

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new MCP tool `unregister` accepts a `session_id` and: deletes all `Application` CRs in the session namespace, deletes all source tarballs for those apps, deletes the session namespace (including all K8s resources within it), and removes the session from the session store
- [ ] Sessions have an optional TTL configured via `IAF_SESSION_TTL` env var (e.g., `24h`); when a session exceeds its TTL with no activity, it is marked expired
- [ ] A background goroutine in the apiserver checks for expired sessions on a configurable interval (`IAF_SESSION_GC_INTERVAL`, default `1h`) and runs the same cleanup logic as `unregister`
- [ ] Session activity is updated on every MCP tool call (any tool call resets the TTL timer)
- [ ] The `SessionStore` gains a `Delete(sessionID string) error` method and a `ListExpired() ([]Session, error)` method
- [ ] If TTL is not set, sessions never expire (preserves current behavior as opt-in)
- [ ] `unregister` returns a summary of what was deleted (app names, namespace name)
- [ ] The `register` tool's response includes the session TTL (if set) so agents know how long they have
- [ ] Tests cover: `unregister` happy path, GC triggering on expired sessions, sessions with activity not being GC'd

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
`unregister` must validate that the `session_id` belongs to an existing session — it must not be possible to call it with a guessed session ID and delete another agent's namespace. The K8s namespace deletion is irreversible; the tool should log the deletion at `WARN` level with full details before executing.

## Out of Scope
- Pausing/hibernating apps rather than deleting them (future)
- Session transfer between agents
- Admin-initiated session termination via the dashboard (future)

## Open Questions
1. Should the GC delete namespaces immediately on TTL expiry, or first scale all deployments to 0 and delete after a grace period?
2. Should `unregister` be callable without a running session (e.g., to clean up a session whose agent has already exited)?

- [ ] Session activity is updated on every MCP tool call (any tool call resets the TTL timer)
- [ ] The `SessionStore` gains a `Delete(sessionID string) error` method and a `ListExpired() ([]Session, error)` method
- [ ] If TTL is not set, sessions never expire (preserves current behavior as opt-in)
- [ ] `unregister` returns a summary of what was deleted (app names, namespace name)
- [ ] The `register` tool's response includes the session TTL (if set) so agents know how long they have
- [ ] Tests cover: `unregister` happy path, GC triggering on expired sessions, sessions with activity not being GC'd

## Security & Multi-tenancy Considerations
`unregister` must validate that the `session_id` belongs to an existing session — it must not be possible to call it with a guessed session ID and delete another agent's namespace. The K8s namespace deletion is irreversible; the tool should log the deletion at `WARN` level with full details before executing.

## Out of Scope
- Pausing/hibernating apps rather than deleting them (future)
- Session transfer between agents
- Admin-initiated session termination via the dashboard (future)

## Open Questions
1. Should the GC delete namespaces immediately on TTL expiry, or first scale all deployments to 0 and delete after a grace period?
2. Should `unregister` be callable without a running session (e.g., to clean up a session whose agent has already exited)?

- Add optional `pod_name` parameter to `app_logs`; validates pod belongs to the app's label selector before fetching (prevents cross-app log access)

content.content:
https://github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/issues/6
author:	dlapiduz
association:	owner
edited:	false
status:	none
--
## Technical Design

### Approach
Three parts: (1) extend `Session` with `LastActivityAt` + `TTL` and add `Delete`, `Touch`, `ListExpired` to `SessionStore`; (2) new `unregister` MCP tool; (3) background GC goroutine in a new `internal/sessiongc` package shared by apiserver and mcpserver. Activity is tracked via `Touch` called in `ResolveNamespace` on every tool call.

### Changes Required

- **`internal/auth/session.go`** — extend `Session` struct with `LastActivityAt time.Time` and `TTL time.Duration` (0 = no expiry). New methods: `Delete(sessionID) error`, `Touch(sessionID)`, `ListExpired() []*Session`, `List() []*Session`. Update `Register(name string, ttl time.Duration) (*Session, error)`.

- **`internal/mcp/tools/deps.go`** — add `SessionTTL time.Duration` to `Dependencies`. Update `ResolveNamespace` to call `deps.Sessions.Touch(sessionID)` after successful lookup.

- **`internal/mcp/tools/register.go`** — pass `deps.SessionTTL` to `sessions.Register`. Add optional `ttl_seconds` and `expires_after` fields to response when TTL > 0.

- **New `internal/mcp/tools/unregister.go`** — `unregister` tool:
  1. Validate session exists via `Lookup`
  2. List apps in namespace (for summary)
  3. `deps.Store.DeleteNamespace(namespace)` — new method removing entire namespace dir
  4. `deps.Client.Delete(ctx, &corev1.Namespace{Name: namespace})` — cascades to all K8s resources
  5. `deps.Sessions.Delete(sessionID)`
  6. Return `{status, namespace, deletedApps, deletedAppCount, message}`
  - Log at `WARN` before namespace deletion (audit trail)

- **`internal/sourcestore/store.go`** — new `DeleteNamespace(namespace string) error` method (`os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(s.dir, namespace))`).

- **New `internal/sessiongc/gc.go`** — `Cleaner` struct with `CleanupSession(ctx, sessionID, namespace)` and `RunGC(ctx)` (calls `ListExpired` then `CleanupSession` for each). `Start(ctx, interval)` runs on a ticker. The `unregister` tool reuses `CleanupSession` to avoid duplicating logic.

- **`internal/config/config.go`** — new `SessionTTL time.Duration` and `SessionGCInterval time.Duration` fields. Env vars: `IAF_SESSION_TTL` (e.g. `"24h"`), `IAF_SESSION_GC_INTERVAL` (e.g. `"1h"`). Default: both 0 (GC disabled).

- **`cmd/apiserver/main.go`** (and `cmd/mcpserver/main.go`) — start `cleaner.Start(ctx, gcInterval)` goroutine if `cfg.SessionTTL > 0`.

- **RBAC** — update `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups="",resources=namespaces` marker to add `delete` verb. Run `make generate`.

- **`internal/mcp/server.go`** — register `RegisterUnregisterTool`; mention in `serverInstructions`.

### Data / API Changes
- New `unregister` tool
- `register` response gains optional `ttl_seconds`, `expires_after` when TTL configured
- `sessions.json` gains `last_activity_at` and `ttl` fields (zero values = no expiry, backwards-compatible)

### Multi-tenancy & Shared Resource Impact
`unregister` and GC both delete only the session's own namespace. Namespace deletion cascades to all K8s resources within it. `CleanupSession` handles `IsNotFound` for idempotency. Concurrent GC runs are safe via the existing mutex pattern.

### Security Considerations
- Session ID is 128-bit random — another agent cannot guess and call `unregister` on a foreign session.
- Namespace deletion logged at WARN before execution.
- `namespaces: delete` RBAC grant is cluster-wide (Kubernetes does not support label-selector restriction on delete). Document and flag for operator review in shared clusters.

### Migration / Compatibility
Existing sessions with `ttl: 0` never expire. Sessions without `last_activity_at` (loaded from old JSON) get zero time — if TTL is enabled on an existing deployment, these sessions will be GC'd on the next tick. Document this migration note.

### Open Questions (resolved)
- GC: immediate deletion, no grace period.
- `unregister` works without active connection (session_id is durable).

### Open Questions for Developer

1→package tools
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→
     7→	iafv1alpha1 "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/api/v1alpha1"
     8→	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/auth"
     9→	iafgithub "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/github"
    10→	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/orgstandards"
    11→	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/sourcestore"
    12→	"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// Dependencies holds shared dependencies for MCP tools.
    16→type Dependencies struct {
    17→	Client       client.Client
    18→	Store        *sourcestore.Store
    19→	BaseDomain   string
    20→	Sessions     *auth.SessionStore
    21→	OrgStandards *orgstandards.Loader
    22→	// GitHub fields — all three must be set for GitHub tools to be registered.
    23→	GitHub      iafgithub.Client
    24→	GitHubToken string // stored but never surfaced in output or logs
    25→	GitHubOrg   string
    26→	// Observability standards — optional override file paths.
    27→	// When empty the embedded defaults in config/org-standards/*.json are used.
    28→	LoggingStandardsFile string
    29→	MetricsStandardsFile string
    30→	TracingStandardsFile string
    31→	// TempoURL is the Grafana base URL used to build traceExploreUrl in
    32→	// app_status responses. Set from IAF_TEMPO_URL. Empty = feature disabled.
    33→	TempoURL string
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ResolveNamespace looks up the session and returns its namespace.
    37→func (d *Dependencies) ResolveNamespace(sessionID string) (string, error) {
    38→	sess, ok := d.Sessions.Lookup(sessionID)
    39→	if !ok {
    40→		return "", fmt.Errorf("session not found, call the register tool first")
    41→	}
    42→	return sess.Namespace, nil
    43→}
    44→
    45→// CheckAppNameAvailable verifies that no application with the given name exists
    46→// in any other namespace. This prevents hostname collisions since all apps
    47→// share the same base domain regardless of namespace.
    48→func (d *Dependencies) CheckAppNameAvailable(ctx context.Context, appName, currentNamespace string) error {
    49→	var allApps iafv1alpha1.ApplicationList
    50→	if err := d.Client.List(ctx, &allApps); err != nil {
    51→		return fmt.Errorf("checking application name availability: %w", err)
    52→	}
    53→	for _, app := range allApps.Items {
    54→		if app.Name == appName && app.Namespace != currentNamespace {
    55→			return fmt.Errorf("application name %q is already in use in namespace %q — choose a different name", appName, app.Namespace)
    56→		}
    57→	}
    58→	return nil
    59→}

    15→
    16→func RegisterRegisterTool(server *gomcp.Server, deps *Dependencies) {
    17→	gomcp.AddTool(server, &gomcp.Tool{
    18→		Name:        "register",
    19→		Description: "CALL THIS FIRST. Creates a new session and returns a session_id that is required by every other tool. You only need to call this once — store the session_id and pass it to all subsequent tool calls. Optionally provide a friendly name for your workspace.",
    20→	}, func(ctx context.Context, req *gomcp.CallToolRequest, input RegisterInput) (*gomcp.CallToolResult, any, error) {
    21→		sess, err := deps.Sessions.Register(input.Name)
    22→		if err != nil {
    23→			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("registering session: %w", err)
    24→		}
    25→
    26→		if err := auth.EnsureNamespace(ctx, deps.Client, sess.Namespace); err != nil {
    27→			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("creating namespace: %w", err)
    28→		}
    29→
    30→		result := map[string]any{
    31→			"session_id": sess.ID,
    32→			"namespace":  sess.Namespace,
    33→			"message":    "Session created. IMPORTANT: Store this session_id and include it in ALL subsequent tool calls as the session_id parameter.",
    34→		}
    35→
    36→		text, _ := json.MarshalIndent(result, "", "  ")
    37→		return &gomcp.CallToolResult{
    38→			Content: []gomcp.Content{&gomcp.TextContent{Text: string(text)}},
    39→		}, nil, nil
    40→	})
    41→}

    13→	APITokens []string `mapstructure:"api_tokens"`
    14→
    15→	// MCP server settings
    16→	MCPTransport string `mapstructure:"mcp_transport"` // "stdio" or "http"
    17→	MCPPort      int    `mapstructure:"mcp_port"`
    18→
    19→	// Kubernetes settings
    20→	DefaultNamespace string `mapstructure:"default_namespace"`
    21→	KubeConfig       string `mapstructure:"kubeconfig"`
    22→
    23→	// kpack settings
    24→	ClusterBuilder string `mapstructure:"cluster_builder"`
    25→	RegistryPrefix string `mapstructure:"registry_prefix"`
    26→
    27→	// Source store settings
    28→	SourceStoreDir string `mapstructure:"source_store_dir"`
    29→	SourceStoreURL string `mapstructure:"source_store_url"`
    30→
    31→	// Routing
    32→	BaseDomain string `mapstructure:"base_domain"`
    33→	// TLSIssuer is the ClusterIssuer name for cert-manager. Default: "selfsigned-issuer".
    34→	// Set to "" to disable TLS certificate provisioning (e.g., cert-manager not installed).
    35→	TLSIssuer string `mapstructure:"tls_issuer"`
    36→
    37→	// Org standards
    38→	OrgStandardsFile string `mapstructure:"org_standards_file"`
    39→
    40→	// GitHub integration (optional — GitHub features are disabled when token is empty)
    41→	GitHubToken string `mapstructure:"github_token"`
    42→	GitHubOrg   string `mapstructure:"github_org"`
    43→
    44→	// Observability (optional — features are disabled when URLs are empty)
    45→	// TempoURL is the Grafana base URL for trace explore links (IAF_TEMPO_URL).
    46→	TempoURL string `mapstructure:"tempo_url"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Load reads configuration from environment variables and defaults.
    50→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    51→	v := viper.New()
    52→
    53→	v.SetDefault("api_port", 8080)
    54→	v.SetDefault("api_tokens", []string{"iaf-dev-key"})
    55→	v.SetDefault("mcp_transport", "stdio")
    56→	v.SetDefault("mcp_port", 8081)
    57→	v.SetDefault("default_namespace", "iaf-apps")
    58→	v.SetDefault("cluster_builder", "iaf-cluster-builder")
    59→	v.SetDefault("registry_prefix", "registry.localhost:5000/iaf")
    60→	v.SetDefault("source_store_dir", "/tmp/iaf-sources")
    61→	v.SetDefault("source_store_url", "http://iaf-source-store.iaf-system.svc.cluster.local")
    62→	v.SetDefault("base_domain", "localhost")
    63→	v.SetDefault("tls_issuer", "")
    64→	v.SetDefault("org_standards_file", "")
    65→	v.SetDefault("github_token", "")
    66→	v.SetDefault("github_org", "")
    67→	v.SetDefault("tempo_url", "")
    68→
    69→	v.SetEnvPrefix("IAF")
    70→	v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))
    71→	v.AutomaticEnv()
    72→
    73→	cfg := &Config{}
    74→	if err := v.Unmarshal(cfg); err != nil {
    75→		return nil, err
    76→	}
    77→	return cfg, nil
    78→}

package mcp

import (
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/auth"
	iafgithub "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/github"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/prompts"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/resources"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/tools"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/orgstandards"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/sourcestore"
	gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
)

// serverInstructions is sent to MCP clients during initialization and injected
// into the LLM's system prompt. This is the primary way agents learn how to
// use IAF.
const serverInstructions = `You are connected to the IAF (Intelligent Application Fabric) MCP server — a platform for deploying applications on Kubernetes.

CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Before calling any other tool, you MUST call the "register" tool to get a session_id. Every other tool requires session_id as a parameter. Without it, all tool calls will fail.

QUICK START:
1. Call "register" (with an optional friendly name) → returns a session_id
2. Call "push_code" with your session_id, an app name, and a map of file paths to contents → builds and deploys your code
3. Call "app_status" with your session_id and app name → check build/deploy progress
4. Once status is "Running", your app is live at http://<app-name>.<base-domain>

AVAILABLE TOOLS (all require session_id except register):
- register: Get a session_id (CALL THIS FIRST)
- push_code: Upload source code files to build and deploy (provide files as {"path": "content"} map)
- deploy_app: Deploy from a container image or git repo (use git_credential for private repos)
- list_apps: See all your deployed apps
- app_status: Check build/deploy progress for an app
- app_logs: View application or build logs
- delete_app: Remove an app and its resources
- add_git_credential: Store a git credential (username/password or SSH key) for private repo access
- list_git_credentials: List stored git credentials (no secrets returned)
- delete_git_credential: Remove a git credential
- list_data_sources: List all platform data sources (databases, APIs, etc.)
- get_data_source: Get details about a specific data source including env var names
- attach_data_source: Attach a data source to your app (injects credentials as env vars)
- provision_service: Provision a managed backing service (e.g. PostgreSQL) — poll service_status every 10s until Ready
- service_status: Check provisioning status; returns connectionEnvVars when Ready
- bind_service: Inject service credentials into an app as K8s Secret references
- unbind_service: Remove service credentials from an app
- deprovision_service: Delete a managed service (must unbind all apps first)
- list_services: List all managed services in your namespace

KEY DETAILS:
- Apps are built automatically using Cloud Native Buildpacks (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Ruby)
- Default port is 8080 — your app must listen on this port
- Your app will be available at http://<app-name>.<base-domain> once Running
- Each session gets its own isolated Kubernetes namespace
- Use app_status to monitor builds (typically ~2 minutes)
- Use app_logs with build_logs=true to debug build failures

CODING STANDARDS:
- Read the coding-guide prompt for organisation coding standards before writing any code
- Read iaf://org/coding-standards for the machine-readable standards document`

// NewServer creates and configures the MCP server with all tools.
// If loader is non-nil, org standards are served from that loader; otherwise platform defaults are used.
// ghClient may be nil — GitHub tools are omitted when it is not set.
// If clientset is non-nil, app_logs will stream real logs from pods.
func NewServer(k8sClient client.Client, sessions *auth.SessionStore, store *sourcestore.Store, baseDomain string, loader *orgstandards.Loader, ghClient iafgithub.Client, ghOrg, ghToken string, tempoURL string, clientset ...kubernetes.Interface) *gomcp.Server {
	server := gomcp.NewServer(
		&gomcp.Implementation{
			Name:    "iaf",
			Version: "0.1.0",
		},
		&gomcp.ServerOptions{
			Instructions: serverInstructions,
		},
	)

	deps := &tools.Dependencies{
		Client:       k8sClient,
		Store:        store,
		BaseDomain:   baseDomain,
		Sessions:     sessions,
		OrgStandards: loader,
		GitHub:       ghClient,
		GitHubOrg:    ghOrg,
		GitHubToken:  ghToken,
		TempoURL:     tempoURL,
	}

	tools.RegisterRegisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeployApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterPushCode(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAddGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListGitCredentials(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAppStatus(server, deps)
	if len(clientset) > 0 && clientset[0] != nil {
		tools.RegisterAppLogsWithClientset(server, deps, clientset[0])
	} else {
		tools.RegisterAppLogs(server, deps)
	}
	tools.RegisterListApps(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeleteApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListDataSources(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterGetDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAttachDataSource(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterProvisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterServiceStatus(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterBindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterUnbindService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeprovisionService(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListServices(server, deps)

	prompts.RegisterDeployGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLanguageGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterCodingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterScaffoldGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterServicesGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterLoggingGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterMetricsGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterTracingGuide(server, deps)

	resources.RegisterPlatformInfo(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLanguageResources(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterApplicationSpec(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterOrgStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterScaffoldResource(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterDataCatalog(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterLoggingStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterMetricsStandards(server, deps)
	resources.RegisterTracingStandards(server, deps)

	// GitHub components — registered only when a token and org are configured.
	if deps.GitHub != nil {
		tools.RegisterSetupGithubRepo(server, deps)
		resources.RegisterGitHubStandards(server, deps)
		prompts.RegisterGitHubGuide(server, deps)
	}

	return server
}

package mcp

import (
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/auth"
	iafgithub "github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/github"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/prompts"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/resources"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/mcp/tools"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/orgstandards"
	"github.com/dlapiduz/iaf/internal/sourcestore"
	gomcp "github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
)

// serverInstructions is sent to MCP clients during initialization and injected
// into the LLM's system prompt. This is the primary way agents learn how to
// use IAF.
const serverInstructions = `You are connected to the IAF (Intelligent Application Fabric) MCP server — a platform for deploying applications on Kubernetes.

CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Before calling any other tool, you MUST call the "register" tool to get a session_id. Every other tool requires session_id as a parameter. Without it, all tool calls will fail.

QUICK START:
1. Call "register" (with an optional friendly name) → returns a session_id
2. Call "push_code" with your session_id, an app name, and a map of file paths to contents → builds and deploys your code
3. Call "app_status" with your session_id and app name → check build/deploy progress
4. Once status is "Running", your app is live at http://<app-name>.<base-domain>

AVAILABLE TOOLS (all require session_id except register):
- register: Get a session_id (CALL THIS FIRST)
- push_code: Upload source code files to build and deploy (provide files as {"path": "content"} map)
- deploy_app: Deploy from a container image or git repo (use git_credential for private repos)
- list_apps: See all your deployed apps
- app_status: Check build/deploy progress for an app
- app_logs: View application or build logs
- delete_app: Remove an app and its resources
- add_git_credential: Store a git credential (username/password or SSH key) for private repo access
- list_git_credentials: List stored git credentials (no secrets returned)
- delete_git_credential: Remove a git credential
- list_data_sources: List all platform data sources (databases, APIs, etc.)
- get_data_source: Get details about a specific data source including env var names
- attach_data_source: Attach a data source to your app (injects credentials as env vars)
- provision_service: Provision a managed backing service (e.g. PostgreSQL) — poll service_status every 10s until Ready
- service_status: Check provisioning status; returns connectionEnvVars when Ready
- bind_service: Inject service credentials into an app as K8s Secret references
- unbind_service: Remove service credentials from an app
- deprovision_service: Delete a managed service (must unbind all apps first)
- list_services: List all managed services in your namespace

KEY DETAILS:
- Apps are built automatically using Cloud Native Buildpacks (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Ruby)
- Default port is 8080 — your app must listen on this port
- Your app will be available at http://<app-name>.<base-domain> once Running
- Each session gets its own isolated Kubernetes namespace
- Use app_status to monitor builds (typically ~2 minutes)
- Use app_logs with build_logs=true to debug build failures

CODING STANDARDS:
- Read the coding-guide prompt for organisation coding standards before writing any code
- Read iaf://org/coding-standards for the machine-readable standards document`

// NewServer creates and configures the MCP server with all tools.
// If loader is non-nil, org standards are served from that loader; otherwise platform defaults are used.
// ghClient may be nil — GitHub tools are omitted when it is not set.
// If clientset is non-nil, app_logs will stream real logs from pods.
func NewServer(k8sClient client.Client, sessions *auth.SessionStore, store *sourcestore.Store, baseDomain string, loader *orgstandards.Loader, ghClient iafgithub.Client, ghOrg, ghToken string, tempoURL string, clientset ...kubernetes.Interface) *gomcp.Server {
	server := gomcp.NewServer(
		&gomcp.Implementation{
			Name:    "iaf",
			Version: "0.1.0",
		},
		&gomcp.ServerOptions{
			Instructions: serverInstructions,
		},
	)

	deps := &tools.Dependencies{
		Client:       k8sClient,
		Store:        store,
		BaseDomain:   baseDomain,
		Sessions:     sessions,
		OrgStandards: loader,
		GitHub:       ghClient,
		GitHubOrg:    ghOrg,
		GitHubToken:  ghToken,
		TempoURL:     tempoURL,
	}

	tools.RegisterRegisterTool(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterDeployApp(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterPushCode(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterAddGitCredential(server, deps)
	tools.RegisterListGitCredentials(server, deps)

	prompts.RegisterScaffoldGuide(server, deps)
	prompts.RegisterServicesGuide(server, deps)
